prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_investigation_candidate repository_module_core)
(attach_diagnostic_tool_to_entity repository_module_core diagnostic_tracer)
(assign_test_case_to_entity repository_module_core test_case_repro)
(prepare_analysis_for_entity repository_module_core test_case_repro)
(bind_issue_marker_to_module repository_module_core issue_marker_101 test_case_repro)
(attach_execution_environment_to_entity repository_module_core execution_env_docker)
(configure_module_for_issue_review repository_module_core execution_env_docker)
(attach_review_note_to_module repository_module_core review_note_initial)
(apply_review_verification repository_module_core review_note_initial)
(finalize_module_verification_after_config_review repository_module_core)
(assign_fix_candidate_to_entity repository_module_core trace_record_1 test_case_repro)
(apply_fix_to_module repository_module_core diagnostic_tracer trace_record_1)
(detach_test_case_from_entity repository_module_core test_case_repro)
(detach_execution_environment_from_entity repository_module_core execution_env_docker)
(detach_review_note_from_module repository_module_core review_note_initial)
(register_investigation_candidate primary_component_a)
(attach_diagnostic_tool_to_entity primary_component_a diagnostic_analyzer)
(assign_test_case_to_entity primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(prepare_analysis_for_entity primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(mark_interface_stable primary_component_a interface_v1 test_case_repro)
(attach_execution_environment_to_entity primary_component_a execution_env_docker)
(finalize_primary_component_ready primary_component_a interface_v1 execution_env_docker)
(detach_test_case_from_entity primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(detach_execution_environment_from_entity primary_component_a execution_env_docker)
(register_investigation_candidate dependent_component_b)
(attach_diagnostic_tool_to_entity dependent_component_b diagnostic_profiler)
(assign_test_case_to_entity dependent_component_b test_case_repro)
(prepare_analysis_for_entity dependent_component_b test_case_repro)
(mark_protocol_state_stable dependent_component_b protocol_state_v1 test_case_repro)
(attach_execution_environment_to_entity dependent_component_b execution_env_docker)
(finalize_dependent_component_ready dependent_component_b protocol_state_v1 execution_env_docker)
(detach_test_case_from_entity dependent_component_b test_case_repro)
(detach_execution_environment_from_entity dependent_component_b execution_env_docker)
(assemble_patch_bundle primary_component_a dependent_component_b interface_v1 protocol_state_v1 patch_bundle_initial)
(assign_test_case_to_entity primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(mark_patch_bundle_tested patch_bundle_initial primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(mark_primary_component_diagnosed primary_component_a patch_bundle_initial)
(mark_dependent_component_diagnosed dependent_component_b patch_bundle_initial)
(detach_test_case_from_entity primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(register_investigation_candidate repository_module_aux)
(attach_diagnostic_tool_to_entity repository_module_aux diagnostic_tracer)
(assign_test_case_to_entity repository_module_aux test_case_repro)
(prepare_analysis_for_entity repository_module_aux test_case_repro)
(execute_test_suite_against_bundle repository_module_aux regression_suite_b patch_bundle_initial)
(flag_module_preparation repository_module_aux regression_suite_b patch_bundle_initial test_case_repro)
(attach_binary_artifact_to_module repository_module_aux binary_artifact_v1)
(attach_execution_environment_to_entity repository_module_aux execution_env_docker)
(mark_module_build_ready_without_protocol_flag repository_module_aux binary_artifact_v1 execution_env_docker regression_suite_b patch_bundle_initial)
(attach_review_note_to_module repository_module_aux review_note_initial)
(start_module_review repository_module_aux review_note_initial regression_suite_b patch_bundle_initial)
(finalize_module_verification_without_label repository_module_aux)
(assign_fix_candidate_to_entity repository_module_aux trace_record_1 test_case_repro)
(apply_fix_to_module repository_module_aux diagnostic_tracer trace_record_1)
(detach_test_case_from_entity repository_module_aux test_case_repro)
(assign_test_case_to_entity primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(assign_fix_candidate_to_entity primary_component_a trace_record_1 test_case_repro)
(apply_fix_to_primary_component primary_component_a diagnostic_analyzer trace_record_1)
(detach_test_case_from_entity primary_component_a test_case_repro)
(assign_test_case_to_entity dependent_component_b test_case_repro)
(assign_fix_candidate_to_entity dependent_component_b trace_record_1 test_case_repro)
(apply_fix_to_dependent_component dependent_component_b diagnostic_profiler trace_record_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a classical planning problem in PDDL for "cross_entity_invariant_repair". I need to output only the executable plan as ground PDDL actions, one per line.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task focuses on a classical planning problem in PDDL, specifically "cross_entity_invariant_repair."  
- The goal is to restore invariants by generating an executable plan.  

## Preparing the Output  
- The output must consist of ground PDDL actions, one per line.  
- Only executable actions should be included, ensuring clarity and precision in the plan.

## Examining Entity Relationships  
- Repair targets include subtypes entity_variant_a and entity_variant_b, with primary_component as entity_variant_a, dependent_component as entity_variant_a, and repository_module as entity_variant_b.  
- All components share the repair_target category and have associated invariants needing restoration, involving one primary, one dependent, and two repository modules.  

## Identifying Diagnostic Requirements  
- To restore invariants, apply_fix_to_... requires fix_assigned, diagnostic_attachment, entity_trace_binding, and the entity not already restored.  
- Fix_assigned comes from assign_fix_candidate_to_entity, which needs diagnosed entity, test_attachment, and trace_record_available.  

## Exploring Diagnosis Processes  
- For components like primary_component_a, diagnosis requires component_interface_evaluated, component_ready, patch_bundle_assembled, and patch_bundle_tested.  
- Repository modules can be diagnosed via finalize_module_verification, with options like without_label, with_final_checks, or after_config_review, depending on conditions.  

## Analyzing Resource Constraints  
- Shared resources include patch_bundle_initial, test_case_available, execution_env_docker, and others, with limited availability like issue_marker_101 but not issue_marker_102.  
- Module_issue_binding exists for both repository modules, but issue_marker_102 being unavailable suggests different repair paths for some entities.

## Analyzing Module Diagnosis Paths  
- For repository modules, diagnosis can follow an issue path, requiring environment_attachment and module_configured before finalizing verification.  
- The review path offers an alternative, starting with module_build_ready and lacking compatibility flags, leading to finalize_module_verification_without_label for diagnosis.

## Checking Resource Availability  
- Review_note and binary_artifact are single resources, potentially limiting parallel paths for core and auxiliary modules.  
- Two test_suites are available, supporting diagnosis for both modules under stable conditions.

## Exploring Build Preparation  
- Module_build_ready requires flag_module_preparation, which depends on executed test_suites and validated bundles, ensuring no compatibility flags.  
- Execute_test_suite_against_bundle sets test_suite_executed and validated, consuming test_suite_availability for each module.

## Preparing Components for Diagnosis  
- Start with registering primary_component_a as an investigation candidate and attaching diagnostic_analyzer.  
- Prepare analysis for primary_component_a using test_case_repro, mark interface stable, and finalize component readiness with execution_env_docker.  

- Detach test_case_repro from primary_component_a to make it available for dependent_component_b.  
- Register dependent_component_b, attach diagnostic_profiler, and prepare analysis, marking protocol state stable before finalizing readiness.  

## Managing Resource Sharing  
- Detach execution_env_docker from primary_component_a to free it for dependent_component_b.  
- Assemble patch_bundle_initial for both components, then test it by reattaching test_case_repro to primary_component_a and marking the patch tested.  

## Diagnosing Components  
- Mark both primary_component_a and dependent_component_b as diagnosed using the tested patch_bundle_initial.  
- Detach test_case_repro from primary_component_a after diagnosis to free resources.  

## Initiating Module Diagnosis  
- Register repository_module_core as a candidate, attach diagnostic_tracer, and prepare analysis with test_case_repro for issue-based diagnosis.  
- Bind issue_marker_101 to repository_module_core, leveraging analysis_prepared and test_attachment for the issue path.

## Assessing Diagnosis Requirements  
- All component paths to interface_evaluated require analysis_prepared, ensuring diagnostic tools are attached for primary_component_a and dependent_component_b.  
- Stabilizing interfaces or restoring test data also depends on analysis_prepared, leaving no alternative without diagnostic attachment.  

## Evaluating Diagnostic Constraints  
- Four entities (primary_a, dependent_b, repo_core, repo_aux) need diagnostics, but only three tools (analyzer, profiler, tracer) are available, causing a resource bottleneck.  
- Diagnostic tools cannot be shared during attachment, as each tool is consumed per entity and remains attached until apply_fix.  

## Exploring Workaround Strategies  
- Considered whether modules like repo_core could bypass investigation_candidate registration, but attach_diagnostic_tool_to_entity requires it, ruling out exceptions.  
- Explored unstable paths or compatibility flags, but they still require analysis_prepared, offering no relief for diagnostic shortages.  

## Proposing Diagnosis Order  
- Realized repo_core, using the config path, can be diagnosed independently without relying on patch_bundle or other components, enabling early diagnosis.  
- Plan to diagnose and apply_fix to repo_core first, releasing its diagnostic tool for use with the remaining entities, resolving the resource constraint.

## Proposing Diagnosis Order  
- Diagnosed repo_core first using the config path, releasing its diagnostic tool for other entities.  
- Plan to apply_fix to repo_core, freeing the diagnostic tool to address the resource constraint for remaining entities.  

## Managing Review Notes  
- Detached review_note_initial from repository_module_core to make it available again.  
- Attached review_note_initial to repository_module_aux for module review, starting with regression_suite_b and patch_bundle_initial.  

## Diagnosing Auxiliary Module  
- Finalized module verification for repository_module_aux without approval, setting verification_flag and diagnosing it.  
- Applied fix to repository_module_aux using diagnostic_tracer, setting it as restored and freeing the tracer.  

## Reallocating Test Cases  
- Detached test_case_repro from repository_module_aux to reassign it to primary_component_a for fixing.  
- Reattached test_case_repro to primary_component_a, then applied fix using diagnostic_analyzer, restoring it and freeing the analyzer.  

## Completing Component Fixes  
- Detached test_case_repro from primary_component_a and reassigned it to dependent_component_b for fixing.  
- Applied fix to dependent_component_b using diagnostic_profiler, restoring it and freeing the profiler, completing all restorations.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
